text: What is the only food that never expires?
funFact: Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible!

text: What country invented pizza?
funFact: The classic Margherita pizza was named after Queen Margherita of Italy in 1889, using tomato, mozzarella, and basil to represent the Italian flag.

text: What fruit is known as the 'king of fruits' due to its strong smell?
funFact: Durian is so smelly it's banned on public transport in Singapore, yet people pay up to £40 for a single fruit!

text: How many teaspoons of sugar are in a can of Coca-Cola?
funFact: That's roughly 39 grams of sugar — more than the entire recommended daily intake for an adult.

text: What is the most stolen food in the world?
funFact: About 4% of all cheese produced globally is stolen. There's even a black market for high-end cheeses!

text: Which country consumes the most chocolate per person?
funFact: The average Swiss person eats about 10kg of chocolate per year — that's roughly 200 chocolate bars!

text: What colour is a ripe coffee bean?
funFact: Coffee beans are actually the seeds inside bright red cherries. They only turn brown after roasting.

text: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
funFact: The word 'guacamole' comes from the Aztec word 'ahuacamolli', which literally means 'avocado sauce'.

text: Which fast food chain has the most locations worldwide?
funFact: Subway has over 37,000 locations worldwide — more than McDonald's, Starbucks, and Burger King combined!

text: What type of pastry is a croissant?
funFact: A traditional croissant has 27 layers of butter and dough, created by folding the dough over and over.

text: What animal can sleep for up to 3 years?
funFact: Snails can sleep for up to 3 years if the weather isn't moist enough for them. Talk about a power nap!

text: How many noses does a slug have?
funFact: Slugs have four noses — well, technically four tentacles. Two are for seeing and two are for smelling.

text: What is a group of flamingos called?
funFact: A flamboyance of flamingos! They're also born grey or white and turn pink from eating shrimp and algae.

text: Which animal's fingerprints are almost identical to humans?
funFact: Koala fingerprints are so similar to human ones that they've actually confused forensic investigators at crime scenes!

text: How long is an elephant pregnant for?
funFact: At 22 months, elephants have the longest pregnancy of any land animal. Baby elephants weigh about 120kg at birth!

text: What is the fastest land animal?
funFact: Cheetahs can go from 0 to 60 mph in just 3 seconds — faster than most sports cars!

text: Which bird can fly backwards?
funFact: Hummingbirds can flap their wings up to 80 times per second and are the only birds that can fly backwards and upside down.

text: What colour is a polar bear's skin underneath its fur?
funFact: Polar bears have black skin to absorb heat, and their fur isn't actually white — it's transparent and hollow!

text: How many legs does a lobster have?
funFact: Lobsters have 10 legs, taste with their feet, and their blood is blue because it contains copper instead of iron.

text: What animal has the longest lifespan?
funFact: Greenland sharks can live for over 400 years. Some alive today were born before Shakespeare was writing plays!

text: In Monopoly, how much money do you collect for passing Go?
funFact: More Monopoly money is printed each year than real money by the US Treasury. The game was originally designed to show the dangers of monopolies!

text: How many squares are on a standard chess board?
funFact: The number of possible chess games is greater than the number of atoms in the observable universe!

text: What colour is the ghost Blinky in Pac-Man?
funFact: Each Pac-Man ghost has a different personality. Blinky (red) directly chases you, while Clyde (orange) actually runs away when he gets too close!

text: In Scrabble, how many points is the letter 'Z' worth?
funFact: The highest-scoring single word ever played in competitive Scrabble was 'QUIXOTRY' for 365 points.

text: What year was the original Mario Bros arcade game released?
funFact: Mario was originally called 'Jumpman' and was a carpenter, not a plumber. He was named after Nintendo's warehouse landlord, Mario Segale.

text: How many pieces does each player start with in chess?
funFact: The word 'checkmate' comes from the Persian phrase 'shah mat', meaning 'the king is dead'.

text: What is the best-selling video game of all time?
funFact: Minecraft has sold over 300 million copies. The entire game was originally created by just one person, Markus 'Notch' Persson, in just 6 days!

text: In Connect Four, how many in a row do you need to win?
funFact: Connect Four has been mathematically solved — the first player can always win with perfect play by starting in the middle column.

text: What shape is the Trivial Pursuit playing piece?
funFact: Trivial Pursuit was invented by two Canadian journalists in 1979 after they realised they were missing pieces from their Scrabble set!

text: In the game Cluedo, what is the name of the victim?
funFact: In the US version, the victim is called 'Mr. Boddy' instead of 'Dr Black'. The game was invented by a British musician during WWII air raid blackouts.

text: What does 'www' stand for in a website address?
funFact: The first ever website is still online! It was created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991 and is just plain text about the World Wide Web project.

text: What was the first message sent over the internet?
funFact: They tried to type 'LOGIN' but the system crashed after just 'LO'. The first internet message was an accidental abbreviation!

text: What year was the first iPhone released?
funFact: The first iPhone had no app store, no copy-paste, and no video recording. It couldn't even send picture messages!

text: What does the 'E' in email stand for?
funFact: The first email was sent in 1971 by Ray Tomlinson. He can't remember what it said — probably something like 'QWERTYUIOP' as a test.

text: Which company created the Android operating system?
funFact: Android was originally designed for digital cameras, not phones. Google bought the company in 2005 for just $50 million.

text: What is the most visited website in the world?
funFact: Google processes over 8.5 billion searches per day. The name 'Google' is a misspelling of 'googol' — the number 1 followed by 100 zeros.

text: How many bits are in a byte?
funFact: A single byte can represent 256 different values. Your average smartphone photo is about 3-5 million bytes (megabytes).

text: What was Twitter's original character limit?
funFact: The 140-character limit was based on SMS text messages (160 chars) minus 20 characters reserved for the username.

text: What does 'USB' stand for?
funFact: It's a running joke that you always need three attempts to plug in a USB. The inventor, Ajay Bhatt, says it annoys him too!

text: In what year was Wikipedia launched?
funFact: Wikipedia has over 60 million articles in 300+ languages, all written by volunteers. If printed, English Wikipedia alone would be over 20,000 volumes.

text: What is the fear of spiders called?
funFact: You're never more than 3 feet from a spider in the UK. But don't worry — most house spiders are completely harmless!

text: How many times does the average person blink per minute?
funFact: That's about 28,800 blinks per day! We blink less when reading or staring at screens, which is why our eyes get dry.

text: What is the most common colour of toilet paper in France?
funFact: Pink toilet paper has been a French bathroom staple since the 1960s. It's considered more aesthetically pleasing!

text: How many dimples does an average golf ball have?
funFact: The dimples create turbulence that reduces drag, making the ball fly nearly twice as far as a smooth ball would.

text: What is the dot over the letter 'i' called?
funFact: The phrase 'to a T' (meaning exactly right) may actually come from 'to a tittle' — down to the smallest detail.

text: Which planet rains diamonds?
funFact: On Saturn, lightning storms turn methane into carbon, which hardens into diamonds as it falls. It literally rains diamonds!

text: How many years is a millennium?
funFact: The word comes from Latin: 'mille' (thousand) + 'annus' (year). The Millennium Dome was built to celebrate the year 2000.

text: What is the longest word you can type using only the top row of a keyboard?
funFact: Some people believe QWERTY keyboards were designed to slow typists down to prevent typewriter jams, but this is actually a myth!

text: What percentage of the Earth's water is fresh water?
funFact: Of that 3%, most is locked in ice caps and glaciers. Less than 1% of all Earth's water is available for us to drink.

text: How many bones does a shark have?
funFact: Sharks have no bones at all — their skeleton is made entirely of cartilage, the same flexible stuff in your nose and ears.

text: Which country has the most time zones?
funFact: France has 12 time zones thanks to its overseas territories scattered across the globe, from the Caribbean to the Pacific.

text: What is the national animal of Scotland?
funFact: Scotland chose the unicorn because in Celtic mythology it symbolised purity, innocence, and power. It's been a Scottish symbol since the 12th century.

text: In which country is it illegal to chew gum?
funFact: Singapore banned gum in 1992 after vandals stuck it on train door sensors, causing delays. You can get fined up to $100,000!

text: What is the most spoken language in the world by total speakers?
funFact: English has about 1.5 billion total speakers, but only 400 million are native speakers. Most English speakers learned it as a second language.

text: Which city is known as the 'Big Apple'?
funFact: The nickname came from 1920s horse racing — 'the big apple' meant the big prize. Jazz musicians later adopted it to mean the big time.

text: What colour are the stars on the European Union flag?
funFact: There are always 12 gold stars on the EU flag regardless of how many countries are members. The number 12 represents completeness and unity.

text: How many stripes are on the American flag?
funFact: The 13 stripes represent the original 13 colonies. A 17-year-old designed the current 50-star flag as a school project — he got a B-minus!

text: What is the currency of Japan?
funFact: Japanese yen coins with a hole in the middle (5 yen) are considered lucky. The word 'go-en' sounds like the Japanese word for good fortune.

text: Which country is home to the kangaroo?
funFact: There are roughly twice as many kangaroos in Australia as there are people — about 50 million kangaroos!

text: What is the tallest building in the world?
funFact: The Burj Khalifa is 828 metres tall — so tall that people on the top floors see the sunset 2-3 minutes later than people at the bottom.
